name: Matarraña-Algars Natural Way Trail
name_original: Pozas del Río Matarraña – circular desde Beceite
description: Jog this moderate 8.2-mile circular route from Beceite, exploring the Matarraña River's natural pools and scenic gorges.
region_intro: Jogging around Beceite offers a diverse landscape characterized by the rugged Puertos de Beceite mountain range, featuring steep limestone hills and Mediterranean forests. The region is crisscrossed by rivers like the Matarraña, Ulldemó, and Pena, which carve impressive canyons and gorges, creating numerous natural pools. Runners can explore varied terrain, from river valleys with wooden walkways to more challenging mountain paths. This area provides a blend of natural beauty and varied elevation suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Beceite The most popular jogging route is Ascent to Penyagalera - circular from Beceite (PR-TE 151), a difficult 11.1 miles (17.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views of the surrounding massif. Another top favourite among local runners is Matarraña-Algars Natural Way Trail, a moderate 8.2 miles (13.2 km) path. This trail allows exploration of natural pools, river beaches, and historical sites along the Matarraña River. Local runners also love the Pena Reservoir – Pantano de Pena loop from Matarraña Nautic, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through river valleys and around the reservoir, often completed in about 53 minutes. Jogging around Beceite is defined by dramatic canyons, crystalline rivers, natural pools, and panoramic mountain views. The network of running routes offers options for various ability levels, from moderate river trails to more demanding mountain ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Beceite's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Beceite?
A: There are nearly 20 dedicated running routes around Beceite listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there running trails suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier jog?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' on komoot, there are 9 moderate routes that are less demanding. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pena Reservoir – Pantano de Pena loop from Matarraña Nautic&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.3 km trail that takes about 53 minutes to complete, offering a scenic and accessible option.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the running routes in Beceite?
A: Beceite's running routes are rich in natural beauty. You'll encounter the rugged limestone mountains of the Puertos de Beceite, Mediterranean forests, and numerous rivers like the Matarraña, Ulldemó, and Pena. Many trails feature impressive canyons, gorges, and over 50 natural pools, such as Las Pesqueras and L'Assut, perfect for a refreshing dip after your run. The spectacular Els Estrets, where the Matarraña River flows through a narrow gorge with vertical rock walls, is a notable highlight.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Beceite area?
A: Yes, many routes in Beceite are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983179859/ascent-to-penyagalera-circular-from-beceite-pr-te-151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Penyagalera - circular from Beceite (PR-TE 151)&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 17.9 km route with panoramic views. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144579" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Racó de Sant Antoni loop from Beceite / Beseit&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 9.5 km trail.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails around Beceite?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in the Beceite region, especially in natural park areas. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or where wildlife is present, and to check specific regulations for individual routes or natural parks like El Parrizal, which might have seasonal restrictions or require dogs to be on a leash.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging routes in Beceite?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' classifications aren't always available, many of the moderate river trails, particularly sections of the Matarraña River paths, are suitable for families. The initial sections of El Parrizal de Beceite, with its wooden walkways, can be a good option, though some parts become more challenging. Always consider the distance and elevation gain when choosing a route for families.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the running routes?
A: Beyond the stunning natural landscapes, you can discover several points of interest. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/688369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyagalera Summit (1,035 m)&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive views. Along the Matarraña River, you might find ancient paper mills and the 18th-century San Bartolomé Parish Church in Beceite. The region also features several caves, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4985583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Glass Cave&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4980827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de la Dona&lt;/a&gt;, which add a historical dimension to your run.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Beceite?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for jogging in Beceite. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended during these months. Winters are cooler but often sunny, making them suitable for running, though some higher elevation trails might be more exposed.

Q: Are there any running routes that feature natural pools for a refreshing dip?
A: Absolutely! The Matarraña River is famous for its crystal-clear natural pools. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983175762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matarraña-Algars Natural Way Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, as it allows exploration of these refreshing spots, including popular ones like Las Pesqueras and L'Assut, which are perfect for cooling down after a run.

Q: How can I access the running trails, especially regarding parking or public transport?
A: Most trailheads around Beceite are accessible by car, and parking is generally available, especially near popular starting points in Beceite village or at specific attractions like El Parrizal. Public transport options to the trailheads are limited, so driving is often the most convenient way to reach many of the running routes. Always check local signage for parking regulations.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Beceite?
A: The running trails in Beceite are highly regarded by the komoot community. Over 100 runners have used komoot to explore the area, praising the diverse terrain, from river valleys to challenging mountain paths, and the stunning natural beauty, including dramatic canyons, crystalline rivers, and panoramic mountain views. The variety of routes ensures there's something for every preference.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Beceite offers several challenging routes for experienced runners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983179859/ascent-to-penyagalera-circular-from-beceite-pr-te-151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Penyagalera - circular from Beceite (PR-TE 151)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 17.9 km trail with significant elevation gain, rewarding runners with extensive views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perigañol – La Caixa loop from Beceite / Beseit&lt;/a&gt;, a 10.6 km route with substantial climbing.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views of the region?
A: Absolutely. The rugged Puertos de Beceite mountain range provides numerous opportunities for breathtaking panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983179859/ascent-to-penyagalera-circular-from-beceite-pr-te-151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Penyagalera - circular from Beceite (PR-TE 151)&lt;/a&gt; is specifically known for its extensive vistas of the surrounding Els Ports massif from the iconic Peñagalera mountain.
tips: Beautiful pools ideal for swimming in summer or simply to relax and rest with the sound of water.
A few meters from the main road, this corner often goes unnoticed, but it is worth making a stop to enjoy its turquoise colors.

The L'Assut Pool is a beautiful natural pool located in Beceite, in the province of Teruel. This pool has been formed by taking advantage of the water from the Matarraña River, creating a unique space to enjoy with the family. Dive into its crystal-clear waters and enjoy a refreshing swim surrounded by nature.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and terrain like for this jogging route?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered moderate for jogging. The terrain is varied, featuring wooden walkways, rocky paths, and sections alongside the river. While some parts are easy, especially along forest tracks, other areas can be slippery or involve loose stones and steep descents. Appropriate footwear is highly recommended.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Pozas del Rio Matarrana circular route?
faqs.answer: There are designated parking areas along a forest track from Beceite, which provide convenient access to the trailhead for this circular route.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the trail. However, as it's located within the Ports de Beseit Natural Park, it's important to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The warmer months are ideal for jogging this route, especially if you plan to enjoy the natural pools. The refreshing waters are a major highlight during summer. However, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, though some sections might be slippery after rain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the Ports de Beseit Natural Park or this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, there is no entrance fee required to access the Ports de Beseit Natural Park or this specific trail. However, during peak seasons or for access to certain restricted areas within the wider Parrizal de Beceite area, specific regulations or small fees for parking or controlled access might apply. It's always advisable to check local regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What notable natural features and highlights can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is renowned for its stunning natural beauty, particularly the crystal-clear natural pools (pozas) of the Matarraña River. You'll pass by impressive rock formations and gorges. Specific highlights include the Rabosa Pool, Pozas del Mas de Lluvia, and L'assut Pool. The landscape also features a rich variety of flora and fauna typical of the region.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: While some parts of the trail are very accessible, especially closer to Beceite, other sections can be more challenging with rocky paths and potential for slippery surfaces. Families with younger children or those seeking an easier experience might consider shorter, more accessible sections rather than the full circular route, which is rated as moderate.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Camino Natural del Matarraña - Algars, Estels del Sud, Moletes de Arany, Senda del Maestrazgo, and the Camí del Parrissal, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on this route?
faqs.answer: The area is home to diverse wildlife. Along the river, you might spot native crabs and trout. In the wider Ports de Beceite region, especially in mountainous sections, there's a chance to see Hispanic goats, vultures, and eagles. The flora includes yew trees and hollies.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenging sections or technical parts to be aware of?
faqs.answer: While generally moderate, some sections, particularly those within the wider Parrizal area like the 'canal de l'Ase', can be more technical and require experience. These might involve challenging terrain, loose stones, or steep descents. If you are not comfortable with such conditions, it's wise to research specific sections or choose alternative paths.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for slippery sections, appropriate footwear with good grip is essential. Light, breathable clothing is recommended, especially in warmer months. Don't forget to bring plenty of water, sun protection, and a swimsuit if you plan to take a dip in the natural pools.
